title: Hillsborough deputies arrest suspect in $20,000 stolen identity jewelry case
summary: A man was arrested two months after being accused of buying $20,000 worth of jewelry using someone else’s identity in Hillsborough County.

title: FDA says Cyclospora test on Taylor Farms lettuce was false positive
summary: Lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms that initially tested positive for Cyclospora was a false positive, according to the Food and Drug Administration.

title: Latest CPU report shows inflation cooled, but experts warn prices are still high
summary: The latest CPI report shows that inflation has cooled; however, experts warn that prices are still high as people struggle to make ends meet.

title: Tate brothers are expected in a Miami court following their arrest and a UK extradition request
summary: Andrew and Tristan Tate are being held in federal detention following their arrest on Saturday, July 18.

title: Woman arrested in killing of 3-year-old girl: Hillsborough deputies
summary: Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office announced Monday the arrest of a 29-year-old woman in the killing of a 3-year-old girl.

Former Scouts fight proposed sale of 604 acres at Sand Hill Scout Reservation
summary: For generations, Sand Hill Reservation has served as a destination for camping, hiking and Scout activities in Hernando County. Now, a proposal involving nearly half of the property has former Scouts fighting to preserve a place they say helped shape their lives.

title: Tropical Depression Two expected to become tropical storm Monday
summary: Tropical Depression Two continues to organize in the Gulf and is expected to strengthen into a tropical storm later Monday as it tracks near Florida's northern Gulf Coast over the next several days.

title: News to Know for July 20
summary: Florida's Back-to-School Sales Tax Holiday begins today. Plus, local fans celebrate Spain's World Cup victory.

title: Susan Solves It: Million-dollar starters
summary: Tampa Bay 28 Reporter Susan El Khoury advises buyers to compare multiple markets and watch inventory trends as housing conditions slowly improve in some areas.

title: Andy Burnham officially takes over as Britain's prime minister
summary: After weeks as leader-in-waiting, Andy Burnham officially became prime minister on Monday during a meeting with King Charles III at Buckingham Palace.

title: US soldiers killed in Iranian attacks were from Hawaii and Texas
summary: The US has identified the soldiers killed in Iranian attacks on an American base in Jordan as 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas.

title: Gas prices hit average of $4 a gallon amid renewed US-Iran attacks
summary: U.S. gas prices jumped to an average of $4 a gallon again Monday as the U.S. and Iran launched more attacks.

title: Ukraine reports multiple deaths after ballistic missile, drone attacks from Russia
summary: Russia fired two missiles and 94 long-range strike drones at the territory of Ukraine overnight, according to Ukraine’s air force.

title: JD and Usha Vance welcome a baby boy, the first child born to a sitting VP in more than 150 years
summary: Vice President JD Vance and his wife, second lady Usha Vance, on Sunday announced the birth of their fourth child, the first born to a sitting vice president in more than 150 years.
